title: Recursive Containers, Stack Parsing, Depth, and Recovery
description: Deeply nested lists overflow recursion, an unmatched container consumes the rest of the file, child offsets drift, and one invalid construct prevents later valid content from rendering. Learners cumulatively produce bounded nested-block parser and recovery trace through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define base case, recursive reduction, returned state, source advancement, and invariant for nested document structures.
Use an explicit open-container stack where depth, continuation, closure, and source ownership need controlled iteration.
Reject or recover from excessive nesting before Python recursion, memory, or diagnostic output becomes unbounded.
